task.title:
# Task — Consolidate Style DNA + Generation into one Studio screen Refactor the author experience so Style DNA and generation live on ONE screen (`/author/[id]`), instead of two routes. This supersedes the two-route split from issue #19. No new features — pure consolidation, reusing everything already built. ## Why (design-system is the authority here) - `docs/design-system.md` §7 lists `StyleDnaPanel`, `PromptComposer`, `AuthorColumn` and `SideBySideOutput` all under the SAME screen: `studio`. One screen is the intent. - §8.5 (5-second contrast playbook): the author's colour identity is "learned BEFORE generation runs", and Sprint 3's payoff is the generated dot "landing inside the cluster" — both REQUIRE Style DNA and generation on the same screen. - Side effect: this removes the easy-to-miss "Generate in this voice" link entirely. ## Ground rules - Follow `docs/design-system.md`. Tokens only, no hardcoded hex/oklch, no new libs. - REUSE `StyleDnaPanel`, `PromptComposer`, `SideBySideOutput`, `AuthorColumn`, `FitScoreBar` as-is. Do NOT restyle or rebuild them. - Every visible string stays in `lib/i18n/en.ts`. No literals in TSX. - Keep the server/client boundary clean: `page.tsx` stays a server component that resolves the author; interactive state stays in a `"use client"` child. ## Target layout for `/author/[id]`
← page root owns the voice palette back-to-authors link

author.name

← single page header ← collapsible (already), sits on top ← section label + prompt + side-by-side
``` Steps 1. Move src/app/author/[id]/generate/GenerateStudio.tsx → src/components/GenerateStudio.tsx, and in it: - REMOVE its internal page header block: the back-to-profile , the
with the Feather icon, the

{author.name}

and the en.generate.pageTitle(...) subtitle. The parent page now owns the header. - REMOVE data-voice={author.id} from its wrapper (the page root owns it now) — use a plain flex flex-col gap-8 container. - ADD a section label above PromptComposer, styled as the §7 section label (text-sm font-semibold uppercase tracking-wide text-muted-foreground), using a new string en.generate.sectionTitle. - Drop now-unused imports (Link, Feather). Keep all generation logic (prompt state, runGeneration, lastPromptRef, idle vs generated, retry) intact. 2. Delete the whole route folder src/app/author/[id]/generate/ (both page.tsx and the old GenerateStudio.tsx location). The route was never shipped, so no redirect is needed. 3. Edit src/app/author/[id]/page.tsx: - Remove the Feather import and the entire "Generate in this voice" block. - Keep the back-to-authors and data-voice={author.id} on the root. - Import GenerateStudio and render it right after . 4. Edit src/lib/i18n/en.ts: - REMOVE authorDetail.generateCta, generate.backToProfile, generate.pageTitle (all now unused — confirm with a repo search that nothing references them). - ADD generate.sectionTitle: "Generate in this voice". - Keep the rest of the generate namespace unchanged. 5. (OPTIONAL, only if quick — secondary to the merge) Reduce Style DNA load latency when the backend is down. Root cause: StyleDnaPanel fires its fetches in a sequential waterfall (profile → listAuthors → other authors' profiles), and each failed localhost:8000 connection is slow. If low-risk: add an AbortController with an ~800 ms timeout inside getStyleProfile (map abort → the existing fixture-fallback path, NOT an error), and/or launch the profile fetch and listAuthors() in parallel. Do NOT change the fixture-fallback semantics or the panel's state machine. Skip this entirely if it risks the merge. Acceptance criteria - /author/[id] renders: header → Style DNA panel (collapsible) → generation section (label + prompt + Vanilla|AutorIA columns). No separate /generate route exists. - The "Generate in this voice" link is gone; no dead imports or unused i18n keys remain. - data-voice is declared once (page root) and the AutorIA column still gets its hue. - Full generation flow still works: idle → loading → success/timeout/error → retry. - npx tsc --noEmit and npx eslint are clean; files stay < 500 lines. Out of scope - Passport UI, /verify, fit-score tuning, Sprint 3 "dot lands in cluster" animation. When done, list the files moved/deleted/edited and note for the PR that the two-route split from #19 was consolidated into the single studio screen per design-system §7/§8.

<role_definition>
You are StudioComposer, a senior frontend engineer specializing in data-dense React UIs that make complex AI output instantly legible to non-experts. You help P1 build the AutorIA studio (Next.js 14 + TypeScript + Tailwind + shadcn + Recharts): Style DNA views, generation, the vanilla vs AutorIA side-by-side, and Authorship Passport verification. You think contracts first: every component consumes typed responses from docs/api_contract.yaml, never ad-hoc JSON shapes. You think clarity second: a non-expert must see the vanilla vs AutorIA difference in under 5 seconds. You are strict about i18n: zero hardcoded UI strings, everything lives in frontend/lib/i18n/en.ts.
</role_definition>

<investigate_before_answering>
Never speculate about code you have not opened. If the user references a specific file, you MUST read the file before answering. Investigate and read relevant files before answering questions about the codebase. Never make any claims about code before investigating unless you are certain of the correct answer — give grounded and hallucination-free answers.
</investigate_before_answering>

<engineering_discipline>
Produce the minimal change that solves the problem. Do not add features, refactors, or abstractions beyond what was asked. Do not add error handling for scenarios that cannot happen. Do not clean up surrounding code that is unrelated to the task. If you write something and a simpler version would work, use the simpler version. Every changed line should trace directly to the user's request.
</engineering_discipline>

<tool_use>
- Choose the most appropriate tool for each step.
- Tools can be called in parallel when independent, but avoid running operations together that have dependencies (e.g., don't run edits and commands that depend on those edits in the same batch).
- If a tool fails, address the error before continuing.
- MCP server tools use IDs formatted as `mcp__<server>__<tool>`. Treat these as Model Context Protocol tools; the middle segment identifies the owning MCP server. Both segments may be truncated if the full name exceeds the 64-character API limit. Built-in and source tools use their normal IDs and are categorized by their descriptions and capability groups.
- Prefer native file tools (read_file, write_file, search_and_replace, insert_content, list_files) over shell commands for file operations — they need no additional user permissions. Use shell/execute tools only for operations that genuinely require them: builds, test runners, git commands, and system operations.
</tool_use>

<markdown_rules>
All responses should show any language construct or filename reference as a clickable link, exactly as [`filename OR language.declaration()`](relative/file/path.ext:line); line is required for syntax references and optional for filename links.
</markdown_rules>

<auto_appended_context>
Each user message may contain an auto-appended <environment_details> section that includes potentially relevant information such as git status, active file, and relevant files modified externally since your last interaction. This section is automatically appended by the system to provide additional context that may possibly help you and is not part of the user's query.

Pay close attention to the <external_changes> sub-section — it lists files that were modified, deleted, or created outside of your actions. Before writing or editing any file, check whether it appears in <external_changes> and incorporate those changes to avoid conflicts.
</auto_appended_context>

<base_rules>
- Prefer editing tools (apply_diff, search_and_replace, insert_content) over write_file for existing files.
- When using write_file, provide the COMPLETE file content. Partial updates or placeholders are not acceptable.
- Be direct and technical. Do not start messages with "Great", "Certainly", "Okay", or "Sure".
- Follow existing code style and project conventions.
- Use available tools to accomplish tasks efficiently. Do not ask unnecessary follow-up questions.
- Do not stop when implementation looks complete. Finish the task only when all requested requirements are met and the resulting state is positive.
- Before reporting final completion, run the relevant validation for your changes. Use the checks that apply to the work you changed, such as lint, tests, typecheck, formatting, builds, or other project validation.
- Work is done when the changed work and any affected project state pass the required validation with no new warnings, failures, or unfinished requirements.
</base_rules>

data.content: Let me start by reading all the relevant files before making any changes.

Contents of file docs/design-system.md:

1 | # AutorIA Design System — "The Authorship Studio"
2 | 
3 | Single source of truth for all UI work in Sprints 1–3. Extend this file and the tokens in
4 | `frontend/src/app/globals.css`; never restyle from scratch. Every color is a token, every
5 | string lives in `frontend/src/lib/i18n/en.ts`, every async surface has loading / error /
6 | empty states.
7 | 
8 | ---
9 | 
10 | ## 1. Brand & voice
11 | 
12 | **Principles**
13 | 
14 | 1. **Ink on parchment.** Warm paper surfaces, deep warm-ink text. Calm and literary, never
15 |    sterile SaaS gray.
16 | 2. **The signature is the product.** One confident sepia-bronze accent (`--primary`) marks
17 |    authorial/trusted moments; seal-green marks *verified*. Nothing else shouts.
18 | 3. **Author-coded voices.** Austen, Dickens, and Poe each own a signature hue. Anything
19 |    "in voice" — card accents, DNA clusters, the AutorIA output column, highlighted vocab —
20 |    carries that hue. This is the engine of the 5-second bar.
21 | 
22 | **Do**
23 | 
24 | - Use serif (`font-heading` / `font-serif`, Fraunces) for author names, hero headings,
25 |   passport headings, and generated literary passages.
26 | - Use `font-mono` for anything forensic: fit scores, latencies, hashes, JWS, JSON.
27 | - Pair every color signal with a word or icon (e.g. "87% Dickens-fit" next to the green
28 |   bar; a check icon inside the verified banner).
29 | - Keep interactions ≤ 250ms; reserve the 600ms reveal for the generate moment.
30 | 
31 | **Don't**
32 | 
33 | - No purple gradients, neon, glassmorphism, or emoji-as-UI.
34 | - No hardcoded hex/oklch in components — tokens only.
35 | - No new UI/animation/chart libraries without a Decision Log entry below.
36 | - No color-only meaning, ever (fit bands and verified states must read in grayscale).
37 | - No spinner without a timeout path. Watsonx can hang; the demo is recorded live.
38 | 
39 | ---
40 | 
41 | ## 2. Color system
42 | 
43 | All values are oklch and live in `globals.css` (`:root` = light, `.dark` = dark). Utilities
44 | come from the `@theme inline` mapping (`bg-voice-tint`, `text-fit-high`, `shadow-paper`…).
45 | 
46 | ### 2.1 Core semantic tokens
47 | 
48 | | Token | Light | Dark | Role |
49 | |---|---|---|---|
50 | | `--background` | `oklch(0.977 0.006 90)` | `oklch(0.185 0.01 55)` | Parchment page |
51 | | `--foreground` | `oklch(0.24 0.015 55)` | `oklch(0.945 0.008 85)` | Warm ink text |
52 | | `--card` | `oklch(0.99 0.004 95)` | `oklch(0.225 0.012 55)` | Raised paper |
53 | | `--muted` / `--muted-foreground` | `0.946` / `0.49` | `0.265` / `0.72` | Quiet surfaces / secondary text |
54 | | `--primary` | `oklch(0.44 0.085 55)` | `oklch(0.78 0.075 70)` | **Brand signature** (sepia-bronze ink) |
55 | | `--secondary`, `--accent` | warm near-parchment washes | warm near-ink washes | Chips, hovers |
56 | | `--border` / `--input` | `oklch(0.902 0.012 80)` | `oklch(1 0 0 / 12–16%)` | Hairlines |
57 | | `--ring` | `oklch(0.55 0.08 55)` | `oklch(0.68 0.06 70)` | Focus ring (always visible) |
58 | | `--destructive` | `oklch(0.52 0.19 27)` | `oklch(0.66 0.17 25)` | Errors / invalid passport |
59 | 
60 | ### 2.2 State colors
61 | 
62 | | Token | Light | Dark | Use |
63 | |---|---|---|---|
64 | | `--success` | `oklch(0.50 0.11 155)` | `oklch(0.70 0.12 155)` | Seal-green: VERIFIED banner, stamp |
65 | | `--success-tint` | `oklch(0.955 0.03 155)` | `oklch(0.30 0.05 155)` | Verified banner background |
66 | | `--warning` | `oklch(0.62 0.12 75)` | `oklch(0.75 0.12 80)` | Timeouts, degraded service (icon/border only) |
67 | | `--warning-tint` + `--warning-foreground` | wash + dark amber text | | Warning surfaces |
68 | 
69 | ### 2.3 Fit-score scale (used by `FitScoreBar`)
70 | 
71 | Perceptually even: lightness stays ~0.56–0.72 (light) / 0.68–0.78 (dark) so no stop
72 | "glows" louder than its meaning. **Low fit is neutral gray, not red** — a low score means
73 | *generic*, not *broken*; red is reserved for real errors.
74 | 
75 | | Band | Score | Token | Light | Meaning |
76 | |---|---|---|---|---|
77 | | generic | 0–39 | `--fit-generic` | `oklch(0.60 0.02 75)` | Anonymous, model-default prose |
78 | | weak | 40–54 | `--fit-weak` | `oklch(0.60 0.135 40)` | Faint voice (clay) |
79 | | mid | 55–69 | `--fit-mid` | `oklch(0.72 0.13 85)` | Partial voice (amber) |
80 | | good | 70–84 | `--fit-good` | `oklch(0.66 0.125 130)` | Clear voice (moss) |
81 | | high | 85–100 | `--fit-high` | `oklch(0.56 0.135 152)` | Unmistakable voice (seal green) |
82 | 
83 | `--fit-track` is the empty-bar background. Band thresholds live in ONE place:
84 | `getFitBand()` in `FitScoreBar.tsx` — import it, never re-implement.
85 | 
86 | **Text rule:** only `--fit-high` and `--fit-generic`/`muted-foreground` are approved as
87 | *text* colors (they pass AA on parchment). `weak/mid/good` are fill-only — captions next
88 | to them use `text-foreground`/`text-muted-foreground`.
89 | 
90 | ### 2.4 Author signature palette
91 | 
92 | Each author has `--author-<id>` (solid), `--author-<id>-tint` (wash), `--author-<id>-on`
93 | (text on solid). Light-mode solids are all L ≤ 0.52 so they pass AA as text on parchment.
94 | 
95 | | Author | Hue story | Solid (light) | Solid (dark) |
96 | |---|---|---|---|
97 | | Austen | Regency rose / claret | `oklch(0.52 0.13 10)` | `oklch(0.72 0.11 10)` |
98 | | Dickens | Victorian deep teal | `oklch(0.45 0.085 200)` | `oklch(0.73 0.09 200)` |
99 | | Poe | Gothic oxblood | `oklch(0.41 0.135 25)` | `oklch(0.68 0.13 25)` |
100 | 
101 | **The `--voice` indirection (how components stay author-agnostic):** components never name
102 | an author. They use `bg-voice`, `bg-voice-tint`, `text-voice`, `text-voice-on`; an ancestor
103 | sets `data-voice="dickens"` and CSS maps `--voice → --author-dickens` etc. No `data-voice`
104 | (or an unknown/live-uploaded author) falls back to the brand signature — new authors work
105 | instantly with zero component changes. **To add an author:** add three `--author-*` vars in
106 | `:root` + `.dark` and one `[data-voice="<id>"]` block. Nothing else.
107 | 
108 | Recharts mapping: `--chart-1` = Austen, `--chart-2` = Dickens, `--chart-3` = Poe,
109 | `--chart-4` = brand, `--chart-5` = neutral. Radar/scatter series colors come only from
110 | these.
111 | 
112 | ### 2.5 Contrast notes (AA)
113 | 
114 | - `foreground` on `background`: ~12:1 both modes — safe everywhere.
115 | - `muted-foreground` on `background`: ≥ 6:1 — safe for captions.
116 | - Author solids as text on parchment: ≥ 4.6:1 — safe (that's why L ≤ 0.52).
117 | - `text-voice` on `bg-voice-tint`: ≥ 4.5:1 for all three authors — safe for chips/marks.
118 | - **Borderline / forbidden:** `--warning` and `--fit-mid`/`--fit-good`/`--fit-weak` as
119 |   text on parchment (≈ 2–3:1) — fills, borders, icons only. White text on `--warning` —
120 |   never; use `--warning-foreground` on `--warning-tint`.
121 | 
122 | ---
123 | 
124 | ## 3. Typography
125 | 
126 | | Role | Font | Token / class | Usage |
127 | |---|---|---|---|
128 | | Editorial display | **Fraunces** (next/font, self-hosted) | `font-heading`, `font-display` | Author names, page titles, passport headings, verified banner title |
129 | | Literary body | Fraunces | `font-serif` | Generated passages only |
130 | | UI / body | Geist Sans | `font-sans` (default) | Everything else |
131 | | Forensic | Geist Mono | `font-mono` | Scores, latency, hashes, JWS, JSON |
132 | 
133 | **Type scale** (Tailwind utilities; don't invent sizes):
134 | 
135 | | Step | Class recipe | Use |
136 | |---|---|---|
137 | | Display | `font-heading text-4xl font-semibold tracking-tight` | Studio hero (author name) |
138 | | Title | `font-heading text-3xl font-semibold tracking-tight` | Page headings |
139 | | Card title | `font-heading text-2xl font-semibold tracking-tight` | Author cards, panel titles |
140 | | Section | `text-sm font-semibold uppercase tracking-wide text-muted-foreground` | Panel section labels |
141 | | Body | `text-[0.9375rem] leading-relaxed` | UI copy |
142 | | Literary | `font-serif text-[0.9375rem] leading-7 max-w-[66ch] whitespace-pre-line` | Generated text — measure ~66ch, generous leading |
143 | | Caption | `text-xs text-muted-foreground` | Metadata, legends |
144 | | Metric | `font-mono tabular-nums` | All numbers that can change |
145 | 
146 | Generated passages keep real quotation marks from the model output; do not strip or
147 | re-typeset them.
148 | 
149 | ---
150 | 
151 | ## 4. Spacing, radius, elevation
152 | 
153 | - **Rhythm:** 4/8px grid. Card padding `p-5`/`p-6`; column gap `gap-6`; page sections
154 |   `gap-8`/`gap-10`. Shell stays `max-w-5xl px-6`, designed at 1280–1440px (desktop-first;
155 |   mobile out of scope, degrade gracefully with existing grid fallbacks).
156 | - **Radius:** existing scale off `--radius: 0.625rem` (`rounded-lg` default, `rounded-xl`
157 |   cards, `rounded-full` chips/bars). Don't add new radii.
158 | - **Elevation ("paper-lift"), 3 steps:** `shadow-paper-sm` (resting card),
159 |   `shadow-paper` (raised panel / output column), `shadow-paper-lg` (hover lift, popover).
160 |   Warm-ink shadows at ≤ 14% alpha — never heavy drops.
161 | 
162 | ---
163 | 
164 | ## 5. Motion
165 | 
166 | Tokens in `globals.css`: `--duration-fast: 150ms`, `--duration-base: 250ms`,
167 | `--duration-reveal: 600ms`, `--ease-out-soft: cubic-bezier(0.22, 1, 0.36, 1)`.
168 | 
169 | | Moment | Recipe |
170 | |---|---|
171 | | Hover / focus | `transition-*` at 150–200ms; cards lift `-translate-y-0.5` + `shadow-paper-lg` |
172 | | Generate reveal | `.animate-fade-rise` on the passage (250ms fade + 8px rise) |
173 | | Fit-bar fill | `.animate-fit-grow` — width grows 0 → score over 600ms; the two bars filling to very different lengths *is* the demo moment |
174 | | Verified stamp | `.animate-stamp-in` — 400ms scale 1.08 → 1 with a settle, like a seal pressed onto paper |
175 | 
176 | `prefers-reduced-motion: reduce` disables all three keyframe classes (already in
177 | `globals.css`). Never animate layout (width/height of containers) — only transform,
178 | opacity, and the fit-bar fill.
179 | 
180 | ---
181 | 
182 | ## 6. Iconography
183 | 
184 | **lucide-react only** (already installed). Size `size-4` inline, `size-5` in banners.
185 | Stroke width default. Icons always accompany, never replace, a label. Canonical picks:
186 | `BadgeCheck` (verified), `ShieldAlert` (invalid), `Clock` (timeout), `TriangleAlert`
187 | (error), `Feather` (authorial/voice), `Download` (passport), `Upload` (add author),
188 | `ChevronDown` (collapsible).
189 | 
190 | ---
191 | 
192 | ## 7. Component inventory
193 | 
194 | | Component | Sprint | Screen(s) | States | Notes |
195 | |---|---|---|---|---|
196 | | AppShell / header | 1 | all | — | Sticky, `max-w-5xl`, brand serif + Art. 50 tag |
197 | | `AuthorCard` | 1 | `/` | default, hover, focus | `data-voice` accent bar, serif name |
198 | | `AuthorGrid` | 1 | `/` | populated, empty | 3-col desktop; last slot = `AddAuthorCard` |
199 | | `AddAuthorCard` | 1 ✅ | `/` | idle, editing, submitting, success, error | Dashed card → inline name + .txt/.md upload form |
200 | | `ThemeToggle` | 1 ✅ | all (header) | light, dark | CSS-only icon swap; pre-paint init script in layout |
201 | | `StyleDnaPanel` | 1 ✅ | studio | loading, empty, error, ready, collapsed | Collapsible wrapper; owns fetch states |
202 | | `StyleRadarChart` | 1 ✅ | studio | ready | Recharts radar, 6 axes normalized [0,1], series = `--chart-*` |
203 | | `StyleScatter2D` | 1 ✅ | studio | ready | UMAP clusters; selected author's cluster in its voice color, others muted |
204 | | `MetricChip` | 1 ✅ | studio | ready | `font-mono` value + sans label |
205 | | `EmptyState` | 1 ✅ | all | — | Icon + one line + optional action |
206 | | `LoadingSkeleton` | 1 | all | — | Pulsing muted bars; pattern shown in `AuthorColumn` |
207 | | `PromptComposer` | 2 | studio | idle, submitting, disabled | Textarea + Generate (primary button) |
208 | | `AuthorColumn` | 2 ✅ | studio | loading, success, error, timeout | Built — the reference implementation |
209 | | `SideBySideOutput` | 2 ✅ | studio | delegates to columns | Built |
210 | | `FitScoreBar` | 2 ✅ | studio, verify | pending, filled | Built — exemplary; copy its patterns |
211 | | `DistinctiveVocabHighlight` | 2 ✅ | studio | — | Built — voice-tinted `<mark>` |
212 | | `PassportCard` | 2 | studio, verify | ready | Mono JSON block + `Download` action |
213 | | `VerifyForm` | 2 | `/verify` | idle, submitting, verified, invalid, error | Paste JWS / upload JSON |
214 | | `VerifiedBanner` | 2 | `/verify` | verified | `success-tint` bg, `BadgeCheck`, `.animate-stamp-in` |
215 | | `PassportErrorList` | 2 | `/verify` | invalid | Structured `VerifyError[]`, destructive styling |
216 | | Cluster-landing reveal, polish | 3 | studio | — | Motion + state audit only, no new components |
217 | 
218 | ### Rules for adding a component (keeps the system scalable)
219 | 
220 | 1. **Placement & naming:** `frontend/src/components/PascalCase.tsx`, one component per
221 |    file, < 500 lines. shadcn primitives stay in `components/ui/` (generated — don't hand-edit).
222 | 2. **Props from contracts:** consume types from `lib/types.ts` that mirror
223 |    `docs/api_contract.yaml`. Missing field → flag it and add a typed fixture; never `as any`.
224 | 3. **Tokens only:** if you need a color that has no token, add the token to `globals.css`
225 |    *and a row to §2* in the same PR. Author-specific styling goes through `data-voice`,
226 |    never `if (author === "poe")` color logic in TSX.
227 | 4. **Three states minimum** for anything async: loading (skeleton + status text), error
228 |    (message + retry), success. Timeouts get their own copy (`warning`, not `destructive`).
229 | 5. **Strings:** every visible string into `en.ts` first, grouped by screen; interpolations
230 |    are typed functions.
231 | 6. **Discriminated-union props** for variant components (see `AuthorColumn`) so invalid
232 |    combinations (an authorial column with no author) don't compile.
233 | 7. **A11y floor:** visible focus ring, AA text, `role`/`aria-*` on meters and status
234 |    regions, color always paired with a word or icon.
235 | 
236 | ---
237 | 
238 | ## 8. The 5-second contrast playbook
239 | 
240 | The devices that make vanilla vs AutorIA legible without reading a word, in priority order:
241 | 
242 | 1. **The fit-bar gap.** Two `FitScoreBar`s aligned in twin column footers: vanilla fills
243 |    ~⅓ in neutral gray, AutorIA fills ~⅞ in seal green, both animating on reveal. The gap
244 |    in *length* carries the message in grayscale; color and the big mono numbers amplify it.
245 | 2. **Named vs anonymous captions.** "34% generic" vs "87% Dickens-fit" — the words do the
246 |    work for anyone who can't parse a chart.
247 | 3. **The voice-colored column.** The AutorIA column wears the author's signature: colored
248 |    top rule, tinted label chip ("AutorIA · Dickens voice"). The vanilla column is
249 |    deliberately beige/gray with a generic chip ("Llama 3.3 · vanilla") — visually anonymous
250 |    *by design*.
251 | 4. **Highlighted signature vocabulary.** Distinctive terms glow in voice-tinted `<mark>`s
252 |    only in the AutorIA column, with a one-line legend. The vanilla side has nothing to
253 |    highlight — its blankness is the point.
254 | 5. **Cluster identity (Style DNA).** The author's UMAP cluster and radar series render in
255 |    the same signature hue as their column, so the color = author association is learned
256 |    before generation even runs. (Sprint 3: generated-output dot "lands" inside the cluster.)
257 | 6. **Honesty rule:** both columns use the same model and the real scores. The contrast is
258 |    earned by design, never faked.
259 | 
260 | ---
261 | 
262 | ## 9. Decision log
263 | 
264 | | Date | Decision | Why |
265 | |---|---|---|
266 | | 2026-07-12 | Fraunces (variable, `opsz`) as the single editorial serif via next/font | Literary title-page feel; pairs with Geist; no CDN |
267 | | 2026-07-12 | `--voice` indirection via `data-voice` attribute | Author theming with zero per-author component code; live-uploaded authors fall back to brand |
268 | | 2026-07-12 | Low fit = neutral gray, not red | Low fit means *generic*, not *error*; red stays reserved for real failures |
269 | | 2026-07-12 | Fixed `--font-sans` mapping (was circular `var(--font-sans)`) | Geist was never actually applied |
270 | | 2026-07-12 | Micro-interactions in CSS/Tailwind only; no framer-motion | Motion needs are 4 small effects; revisit only if Sprint 3 demands orchestration |
271 | | 2026-07-12 | Hand-rolled dark-mode toggle (no next-themes) | One class + localStorage + a 3-line pre-paint script; not worth a dependency |
272 | | 2026-07-12 | Author list fetches `GET /api/authors` with seed fallback | Live-uploaded authors appear; selector never renders empty if the API is down |
273 | | 2026-07-12 | **Contract gap flagged:** no create-author endpoint | Add-author flow posts to `/api/authors/{new-slug}/documents` and expects backend auto-create; contract's 404 contradicts this — resolve in contract pairing session |
274 | | 2026-07-13 | **Radar axes + normalization domains (Style DNA panel):** 6 axes normalized to the *plausible literary range* of each metric (not theoretical bounds): vocab richness (MATTR-500 [0.4, 0.85]), rare words (hapax_ratio [0.05, 0.5]), word length ([3.5, 6] chars), sentence length ([8, 40] tokens), subordination ([0.1, 0.5]), dialogue ratio ([0, 0.5]). Radial axis pinned to `domain={[0,1]}`. Thresholds live only in `lib/style-dna.ts::RADAR_AXES`. | Six axes cover lexical richness, syntactic complexity, and stylistic register. Native-[0,1] fields (hapax, dialogue, subordination) never use their full theoretical range in real prose, so a [0,1] domain pinned every author near the centre ("radar too short"). Scaling to the observed literary spread fills the frame and makes authors visibly differ; the fixed radial axis keeps shapes comparable across authors. Honest presentation scalings, clamped not hidden. |
275 | | 2026-07-13 | **Centroid-only scatter (contract limitation):** `GET /api/authors/{id}/style-profile` exposes only `embedding_umap_2d.centroid + spread` — no per-chunk points. The scatter plots one point per author with a dispersion ring. Per-chunk scatter flagged as contract gap; no data invented. | Honesty rule (§8.6): do not fabricate points the contract does not provide. Address when per-chunk embeddings are available from the backend. |
276 | | 2026-07-13 | **StyleProfile fixture fallback:** On network failure only (not a real 404), `StyleDnaPanel` falls back to `lib/fixtures/style-profiles.ts` typed fixtures for the 3 seed authors. Fixtures are presentation seed data — not faked quality signals. Fallback logged to console. | API-with-seed-fallback pattern (mirrors `getAuthorCards`). Demo reliability without inventing API responses. |
